to all sorts of other businesses who need content. If I was local, I would be very alert to any and every other kind of business putting out their own customer newsletter because they're lazy and they need content. I'll provide them content. I do that nationally. And so I'm happy to have books excerpted. We provide articles. We provide fake interviews where I give you a four-page interview about a topic or about my latest book. And here's all the answers and here's the holes for the questions, write your questions and feel free to use it. All that creates this upstream downstream movement of your customers seeing me here, here, here, here. The smart ones say to themselves at some point, gee, he's here, here, here, here, here. Maybe I should swim upstream from those places and take a look at him. And again, online, this is ultra simple. You guess blog posts, guess newsletter articles online, all of that. I mean, it's really simple to do. In your manual, there is an actual referral letter as well that you can take and that you can use to send out. Now talk a little bit about the endorse mailing. Well, the endorse mailing is a very direct way to do this. And sometimes it requires reciprocity, in which case you have to think about whether or not you're willing to do that. Sometimes it doesn't. It is often done through a customer, client or patient. But so you have to be alert to customers, clients or patients who are centers of influence, who have some population of people more than an ordinary customer has, a dozen close neighbors, friends and coworkers, and has maybe a total universe of 50 or so. There are centers of influence who have hundreds and hundreds and hundreds who know them. So the example that's in the manual, at least one of the examples that it's in the manual, is of the real estate agent. So I think I don't think I have it. I put it away because it was in the manual. But so the example in there is of a dentist getting a real estate agent to do the endorsed mailing. And so the real estate agent, who is the patient of the dentist, has two really great centers of influence. So there you go. Thank you. So one is his clients. So your fiance, not now, but will eventually have hundreds and hundreds of clients that she has found a house for or sold a house for. Certainly the ones she's found the house for are still in the area and she'll be maintaining contact with them. So she has that circle of influence. The other circle of influence she has is all the other real estate agents. Peer to peer. And so there's two versions of the same endorsed mailing letter in here, which is for my valued clients, why is a realtor writing to you about a dentist? Dear friend, and then here's why. And this drives by the way to a special report. So it doesn't drive to the dentist. I'm not suggesting you hurry into my dentist. You probably have one. But maybe like me, you've been postponing and procrastinating over some needed care, regardless with no obligation of any kind. Dr. Gummy Bureau will be happy to send you copies of his book on dental health title and his special report, How to Look 10 Years Younger and 10 Pounds Thinner, and the information kit about his practice. I've been teaching this, endorsed mailing for years, and this is the first time I've heard having it go to the report. Well, it makes it easier to get him to do it. Then you put him into your lead generation funnel and it takes over. Absolutely. And then the version of it that goes to the customer is nearly the same, or the agents, for my fellow agents. Why is a realtor writing to you about a dentist? And there's some reason, you know, at the top. That's one is you and I know what being of service is all about. Every once in a while, all two seldom. You get such exceptional service from somebody that you just have to brag about them. But the drive is to the report. The drive is not to go in and get your teeth pried on, right? Brilliant. That's awesome. And so, when you drive to information, it's easier to get somebody to agree to do it. Sure. That it is if you're driving direct to the practice. Number one. Number two, you'll get more response. Right. All of this is as an aside, it's sort of forcing you to be in the lead generation.
